title: EXPLAINED: How Oscar Piastri's Crash Unfolded - 2026 F1 Australian GP Melbourne
description:
EXPLAINED: How Oscar Piastri's Crash Unfolded - 2026 F1 Australian GP Melbourne.

Oscar Piastri’s crash during the reconnaissance laps at the 2026 Australian Grand Prix caught a lot of attention — but incidents like this reveal far more than just a mistake.

In this video, I break down what likely happened in the moments leading up to the crash at Albert Park Circuit from both a technical driving perspective and the mental side of an F1 driver preparing for a race session.

title: 2026 F1 Engine & Battery Deployement Explained (MGU-K)
description:
F1 2026 just introduced one tiny word in the regulations — “pending” — and it completely changes how drivers use the throttle on corner exit.

In this video I break down what a power-limited pending period actually means in driver language, why the car no longer gives a sudden hybrid “kick”, and how the 150kW rule forces drivers to commit to throttle earlier instead of waiting for a boost moment.

You’ll learn:
• What “power-limited” vs “traction-limited” really feels like in the car
• Why the b

These are more than cars. They’re science experiments.
If you enjoy this episode, subscribe to support more optimistic stories about tech!

title: Juan Pablo Montoya's record-breaking lap of Monza | 2004 Italian GP
description:
Colombia's Juan Pablo Montoya averaged an eye-watering 259.83 km/h when setting his pole position lap around Monza back in 2004.

This stood as a record until 2018, when it was narrowly eclipsed by Kimi Raikkonen with an average speed of 263.587km/h: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q5_fo16E4LU

Lewis Hamilton then lowered the record further in 2020, with a lap that averaged 264.363 km/h: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2f1PtJV0vIs

Five years later, Max Verstappen went even faster, with an avera
